mapping.a_element: minimum spanning tree built by Kruskal's greedy algorithm on a randomly weighted graph
mapping.b_element: minimum spanning tree method used to group particles into clusters
fragments extracted from the MST clusterization

brief_justification: Both papers invoke the minimum-spanning-tree object, but that shared name is where the correspondence stops: paper A's actual machinery is a statistical-physics scaling theory of the random MST via its mapping of Kruskal's greedy algorithm to bond percolation, yielding fractal dimension, critical dimension d_c=6, and spin-glass ground-state counting, whereas paper B uses the MST merely as a clustering heuristic and adds a nuclear binding-energy check (modified Bethe-Weizsacker) to decide which clusters are bound fragments. There is no percolation/critical-scaling analysis in B and no binding-energy physics in A, so beyond the generic MST construction no role-for-role mapping of the driving mechanisms holds.
